title: [v5.8] Artifact add optimization on macOS and Windows
body:
This PR adds a new local artifact add API endpoint and enforces absolute path requirements for local file operations.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-3385
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26321

# Benchmark
- Command: `podman artifact add quay.io/myartifact/benchmark:latest ./artifacts/random-file`
- Artifacts Dir content:
```
$ du -a -h | sort -hr
5G	.
5.0G ./random-file
```

## Benchmark Results: 

### Mac OS
- Number of runs: 10
- Machine: 
   - **Chip**: Apple M3 Pro
   - **RAM**: 36 GB
   - **SSD**: 1 TB Apple SSD 
- Podman machine configuration: 
   - **CPUs**: 8
   - **Memory**: 8.1 GB
- [report](https://gcsweb-ci.apps.ci.l2s4.p1.openshiftapps.com/gcs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/pull/openshift-psap_topsail/856/pull-ci-openshift-psap-topsail-main-cont_bench-jump-ci/1986356418457899008/artifacts/jump-ci/005-test/artifacts/test-artifacts/001__plots/report_01_report:_benchmark_comparison_report.html)

#### Providers
##### applehv
- Before: 32.82 s
- After: **5.77 s**

##### libkrun (krunkit 0.2.1)
- Before: 25.87 s
- After: **7.17 s**

### Windows
- Windows 10
- Number of runs: 10
- Machine: 
   - **CPU**: Intel Core i7-8665U
   - **RAM**: 32 GB
   - **SSD**: 500 GB Samsung 970 Evo Plus
- Podman machine configuration: 
   - **CPUs**: 8
   - **Memory**: 2 GB (WSL) | 8.2 GB (Hyper-v)
- [report](https://gcsweb-ci.apps.ci.l2s4.p1.openshiftapps.com/gcs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/pull/openshift-psap_topsail/856/pull-ci-openshift-psap-topsail-main-cont_bench-jump-ci/1985639683794669568/artifacts/jump-ci/005-test/artifacts/test-artifacts/001__plots/report_01_report:_benchmark_comparison_report.html)

#### Providers
##### WSL
- **Memory**: 2 GB
- Before: **35.25 s**
- After: 78.31 s

##### Hyper-V 
- **Memory**: 8.2 GB
- Before: 362.70 s
- After: **51.66 s**

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
Added new local artifact add API endpoint (`libpod/local/artifacts/add`) for loading artifacts from local files
Image and artifact load endpoints now require absolute paths for local file operations to improve security and prevent path ambiguity
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@Honny1 I see the [5.8] tag, but it looks like this is headed to the v5.7 branch?

@Honny1 sorry, I'm a bit confused by this.  This PR doesn't fix #26321, right?  I think that was fixed by #27426 already.  I think this is meant to backport the commits from #26321 into the v5.7 branch, and not the [v5.8] branch as the title says.  Or have I now lost all my marbles.

The backport looks fine, but I do think you mean this for the v5.8 branch, and it looks like it's headed to the v5.7.

I'm not quite clear what process you used for backporting, but @Luap99 pointed me at `git cherry-pick -x {commitID}` and it works great.  If you're not using that, I'd suggest you look into it.

yep this needs to be target against v5.8 branch now, you should be able to switch that in an existing PR a the top. In any case I recommend a rebase on the v5.8 base just to ensure there are no unwanted conflicts in the meantime

> @Honny1 sorry, I'm a bit confused by this. This PR doesn't fix #26321, right? I think that was fixed by #27426 already. I think this is meant to backport the commits from #26321 into the v5.7 branch, and not the [v5.8] branch as the title says. Or have I now lost all my marbles.

I split that into two parts: build optimization and artifact addition optimization. I managed to finish the build optimization before the 5.7 release.
I rebased on v5.8 and changed the target branch. PTAL @TomSweeneyRedHat @Luap99 @mheon
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
